events.title: jailCTF 2026
events.description:
This is the third jailCTF event. Unlike traditional CTFs, this event has a large focus on "jail" challenges which ask players to escape out of a code sandbox with some set of restrictions applied. Categories here aren't traditional here either, with pyjail, esolang, and other mainstream languages as categories (js, perl, ruby, c, etc). There will also be King-of-the-hill (koth) style challenges! Come and have fun in jailCTF!

Discord: https://discord.gg/JxtuFxMaE8

Infra sponsored by https://goo.gle/ctfsponsorship

Additional sponsors for this event, if any, are TBA

events.description:
L3akCTF 2026 is the third annual Capture the Flag competition hosted by team L3ak, welcoming participants of all backgrounds and experience levels.

We’re bringing 50+ original challenges spanning 8 categories: crypto, web, rev, pwn, misc, forensics, OSINT, and hardware/RF. The difficulty will range from beginner to expert.

events.title: Universal CTF
events.prize: Infomaniak: 1st place - 1'000 CHF of Public Cloud credits, valid one year 2nd place - 1'000 CHF of P
events.description: U-CTF, short for Universal CTF, is a jeopardy-style competition set across the outer lanes: the transport corridors, relay routes, stations, and inhabited worlds that keep civilization connected. Teams are recruited as operator units to prove that the system is inherently flawed in every part. Their targets are vital pieces of infrastructure that serve, or once served, every citizen, from training stations and relays to maintenance networks and operational grids. Every challenge is a fault line in the machinery that holds the system together. Your task is to find it and break through it.

events.title: AEROSPACE VILLAGE STARPWN CTF
events.description:
STARPWN CTF 2026
A cyberpunk CTF brought to you by the AEROSPACE Village at DEFCON 34 

Want to try your hand at hacking satellites, spacecraft and ground control systems?
Miss out on Hack-A-Sat?
Want to explore the final frontier of cybersecurity? 
STARPWN is the official Aerospace Village space hacking CTF! Back door flight computers, trojan flight software, exploit CVE's, reverse protocols, and more! During your space hacking journey, you’ll learn all about the environmental and operational constraints of space systems, how they affect cybersecurity posture, and impact exploitability.

Brought to you by:
Aerospace Village
Aerospace Corporation
Ethos Labs
Final Frontier Security
Visionspace
Sixgen
Cal Poly - California Cybersecurity Institute
CTFd

events.title: RoboHack AI CTF (Robotic Hacking Community at DEFCON 34)
events.description:
RoboHack AI CTF is an experimental Capture The Flag event hosted by the Robotic Hacking Community at DEF CON 34.
The CTF target will be an AI robot inside a simulated environment. Instead of requiring continuous manual player interaction, the competition may allow participants to submit their own AI models or agents, which will then be used to interact with, exploit, or solve challenges against the robotic target environment.
The challenge scope may include both AI model security and robotics system security. In addition to allowing participants to compete using AI models, the target systems and challenges themselves may involve AI models such as VLA, VLM, or LLM components. Potential challenge topics may include adversarial patches, prompt injection, prompt jailbreaks, model poisoning, and other attacks against AI-enabled robotic decision-making systems.
The CTF may also include challenges related to the robot’s underlying software stack, communication layers, and robotics protocols, such as ROS 2, DDS, and related robotic middleware or system interfaces.
The goal is to explore the intersection of robotics security, AI model behavior, autonomous exploitation, and CTF-style challenge design. Participants may be asked to think beyond traditional web, pwn, reversing, or cloud challenges and consider how autonomous systems perceive, decide, communicate, and act inside a robotic environment.
This event is expected to be experimental and community-driven. Details such as rules, scoring, participation format, model submission requirements, challenge categories, target environments, and challenge availability may change as the event develops.
Please refer to the official Robotic Hacking Community website for the latest and most accurate event information.
Tentative Challenge Areas
AI robotic security
Autonomous agent / AI model participation
VLA, VLM, and LLM security
Adversarial patch attacks
Prompt injection
Prompt jailbreak
Model poisoning
AI-enabled robotic decision-making
ROS 2 / DDS security
Robotic middleware and communication protocols
Simulated robotic target environments

events.title: HackHowl 2026
events.prize: Top 3 Teams – ThunderCipher VAPT Certification Exam Voucher (one per member) All Participants – 40%
events.description:
HackHowl 2026

HackHowl is an ethical hacking competition. Starting with a 24-hour CTF where every category is locked until you crack the first challenge, then shifting into a full Bug Bounty phase where you'll hunt real vulnerabilities in a corporate infrastructure.

CTF: August 14–15 | Bug Bounty: August 16 (UTC−6)
Teams of 1–5 people | Only 40 teams
Completely free to enter

CTF Categories: Web, Reversing, OSINT, Crypto & Blockchain
Bug Bounty Phase: Hunt real vulnerabilities in a corporate infrastructure

Prizes:
- Top 3 teams: ThunderCipher VAPT Certification Exam Voucher (one per member)
- All participants: 40% discount on ThunderCipher VAPT Exam + Certificate of Participation

Sponsored by ThunderCipher | https://thundercipher.in

Registration: June 25 – July 31 | Only 40 spots available
Register at https://hackhowl.com

events.title: THJCC CTF 2026 summer
events.prize: 1st. 3000 NTD + a THJCC EasyCard + a certificate 2nd. 2000 NTD + a THJCC EasyCard + a certificate 3r
events.description:
THJCC CTF is a cybersecurity competition tailored specifically for high school and vocational school students in Taiwan. It is co-hosted by the SCINT (Northern Taiwan Student Information Community) and like-minded students nationwide, and has received the support of multiple domestic cybersecurity organizations and institutions. Continuing the tradition of previous events, we are specially hosting the THJCC CTF 3rd Summer Edition this summer, hoping to bring students a cybersecurity feast full of challenges and fun during their vacation. This competition maintains the Jeopardy format, with challenges covering Web, Reverse, Crypto, Pwn, and Misc. The difficulty level balances introductory learning for novices and advanced challenges, ensuring that everyone—from beginners just starting in cybersecurity to experienced competitors—can find an opportunity to showcase their skills here. Our core mission is to promote cybersecurity education and cultivate high school students' keen interest and practical abilities in cybersecurity technology. Beyond the competition, participants in the student division have the opportunity to win prize money or other special rewards, with the hope of boosting their confidence in tackling various future competitions. THJCC CTF is not merely a stage for competition, but also a platform for students across Taiwan to learn from each other, hone their skills, and exchange ideas. 

Official website: https://thjcc.org/
Discord: https://dc.thjcc.org/

events.title: NullOrigin CTF Qualifiers
events.prize: Top-performing teams will receive: • Qualification to NullOrigin CTF 2026 Finals • CPPT (Certified P
events.description:
NullOrigin CTF 2026 Qualifiers is the official qualification round for NullOrigin CTF 2026, an international online Capture The Flag (CTF) competition organized by Cyber HX.

The qualifier is a 12-hour Jeopardy-style CTF where teams of 1–4 members will solve real-world cybersecurity challenges across Web Security, Cryptography, Reverse Engineering, Digital Forensics, OSINT, Binary Exploitation, and Miscellaneous categories.

The competition is open to students, professionals, and cybersecurity enthusiasts from around the world. Whether you are just starting your cybersecurity journey or already have CTF experience, NullOrigin offers challenges for every skill level.

Top-performing teams will qualify for the NullOrigin CTF 2026 Finals.

Rewards include qualification to the Finals, a CPPT (Certified Practical Penetration Tester) Certificate by Cyber HX (Worth $99), Exclusive MITRE ATT&CK Lab Access by Cyber HX, internship opportunities, digital certificates, and sponsor rewards.

Mode: Online
Duration: 12 Hours
Team Size: 1–4 Members

Website: https://nullorigin.cyberhx.com

Contact: [nullorigin@cyberhx.com](mailto:nullorigin@cyberhx.com)

events.title: CSAW CTF Qualification Round 2026
events.description: CSAW CTF is a entry-level CTF, designed for undergraduate students who are trying to break into security. Challenges are specifically designed to point students in directions that will help them understand fundamental concepts and develop practical skills. Our sponsors are big players in the security field, who are serious about hiring the right people with the right skills. Our judges are world-renowned experts in the security field, who are dedicated to making sure that our challenges are designed to test these skills.

events.title: WATCHLIST
events.description:
WATCHLIST is a 24-hour online Jeopardy CTF about digital investigation: forensics, OSINT, recon, and the hands-on work investigators actually do. It's a love letter to Person of Interest, narrated by The Machine.

There are 24 challenges across 6 categories: Forensics, Lab, OSINT, Recon, Trivia, and a Person of Interest story track that unlocks as you play.

It's made for beginners through intermediate players, so there's an easy way in and the difficulty climbs from there. Every challenge teaches a real, usable skill with tools people actually use, and the hints are written to teach you something, not just hand over the answer.

A few quick things:

Any tools are fine, including AI. They're helpers, not shortcuts, and they won't finish a challenge for you.
Teams of 1 to 6, and solo players are very welcome.
Free to play.

Prizes: $750 / $500 / $250 for the top three.

When: 19 September 2026, 03:30 UTC. Runs for 24 hours.

Briefing: https://xposedornot.com/ctf
Platform: https://ctf.xposedornot.com
Discord: https://discord.com/invite/X5DtMQgbdP

Updates: https://x.com/@watchlistctf on X

events.prize: Trophies are given to the top 3 teams (if US-based)
events.description:
Crimson Defense CTF 2026 will be held on Saturday, October 3rd from 10am to 10pm CDT!

CDCTF is a virtual 12-hour Jeopardy-style Capture-the-Flag competition designed for collegiate-level players. All ability levels are welcome. 

This competition will not only provide satisfying challenges for seasoned players, but be a good introduction to CTFs for new ones!

CDCTF is a team competition, you may have up to four participants per team. Universities may have multiple teams representing their school.

There will be challenges of all difficulties representing the following categories: Reverse Engineering, Log & Network Traffic Analysis, Forensics, Password Cracking, Cryptography, Open Source Intelligence, Social Engineering, and AI!

Registration will open in August.

CDCTF is hosted and created by Crimson Defense Cyber Security Club at the University of Alabama.

events.title: Platypwn 2026
events.description:
The Platypwn 2026 is an online Jeopardy CTF which offers easy challenges for beginners, but also medium and difficult challenges for advanced players.

See https://platypwnies.de/events/platypwn for further details.

events.title: GlacierCTF 2026
events.description:
We're excited to announce GlacierCTF 2026 for its fifth edition, a 24-hour jeopardy CTF organized by LosFuzzys.

Join our discord at:
https://discord.gg/DFYnRAME23

Infra sponsored by goo.gle/ctfsponsorship

events.title: SwampCTF 2026
events.description: SwampCTF is a student-run competition and hosted by the University of Florida Student InfoSec Team (UFSIT). Join us online for jeopardy-style fun with challenges in Pwn, Reverse, Forensics, Crypto, and Misc. categories.

events.title: ASIS CTF Finals 2026
events.description: ASIS CTF Finals 2026
